etymology.description: 甘(甘) (gān, "sweet") + 匹(匹) (pǐ, "match") = to exceed a matching level -> very
etymology.interpretation: Sweetness (甘) that surpasses a standard match (匹) leads to the concept of 'very' or 'extremely', emphasizing a degree that exceeds the usual measure.

examples.chinese: 这件事让我甚为感动(這件事讓我甚為感動)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiàn shì ràng wǒ shèn wéi gǎndòng.
examples.english: This matter moved me very much.

examples.chinese: 他的才华甚是出众(他的才華甚是出眾)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de cáihuá shèn shì chūzhòng.

examples.chinese: 他对这种情况感到甚为不满(他對這種情況感到甚為不滿)。
examples.pinyin: Tā duì zhè zhǒng qíngkuàng gǎndào shèn wéi bùmǎn.
examples.english: He feels very dissatisfied with this situation.
